name: taoverse

metadata.name: taoverse
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A utilities library for model training subnets.

documentation:
# Taoverse Package

This is a package containing various python modules for use in bittensor subnets.

Currently it is primarily supporting the Macrocosmos SN 9 Pretraining and SN 37 Finetuning subnets but much of the code is generally useful and all are welcome to leverage it for their own work as well.

Find the latest published package here: https://pypi.org/project/taoverse/

Following is an overview of each module and some of the bigger pieces of code they include.

## Metagraph

The metagraph module contains code relating to metagraph operations.

- The `MetagraphSyncer` allows for easily refreshing a specified metagraph at a specified cadence. It will operate in asynchronously on the asyncio event loop and will notify any registered listeners after each sync.

- The `MinerIterator` provides a thread safe infinite iterator that safely handles adding new uids.

## Model

The model module contains code relating to large language models.

- The `ModelTracker` will track model metadata and evaluation history on a per hotkey basis. It is designed to keep state across restarts and has methods to save and load state.

- The `ModelUpdater` reads metadata from the chain, validates it, and checks if it is an update to a previous tracked hotkey.

- The model.competition module supports grouping models into distinct competitions.
  - The `CompetitionTracker` helps track weights at a per competition and per subnet level. Like the `ModelTracker` it is designed to keep state across restarts.
  - The `EpislonFunc` allows for computing epsilon with a custom function on a per competition basis. Both a `FixedEpsilon` and a `LinearDecay` implementation are included.

- The model.storage module supports storing and retrieving models and metadata.
  - For models there are included implementations for Hugging Face and the local disk.
  - For metadata there is an included implementation using the Bittensor chain.

## Utilities

The utilities module contains code found to be generically useful in the context of subnets.

- The `PerfMonitor` is a context manager that tracks the performance of a block of code by taking several samples
- In `utils.py` there are some helpers to `run_in_thread` or `run_in_subprocess`.
  - Running metagraph operations in a separate thread with a ttl can help avoid getting stuck on those actions.
  - Running model evaluation within a sub process can help ensure that the model is completely removed from the gpu afterwards.

functions.name: taoverse.metagraph.utils.assert_registered
documentation:
Asserts the wallet is a registered miner and returns the miner's UID.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the wallet is not registered.

functions.name: taoverse.metagraph.utils.get_top_miners
documentation:
Returns the set of top miners, chosen based on weights set on the valis above the specifed threshold.

Args:
    metagraph (bt.metagraph): Metagraph to use. Must not be lite.
    min_vali_stake (int): Minimum stake threshold for a vali's weights to be considered.
    min_miner_weight_percent (float): Minimum weight on a vali for the miner to count as a top miner.

functions.name: taoverse.metagraph.utils.get_high_stake_validators
documentation: Returns a set of validators at or above the specified stake threshold for the subnet

functions.name: taoverse.metagraph.utils.get_hash_of_sync_block
documentation: Returns the hash of the most recent block that is a multiple of the sync cadence.

classes.name: taoverse.metagraph.miner_iterator.MinerIterator

methods.name: taoverse.metagraph.miner_iterator.MinerIterator.peek
documentation: Returns the next miner UID without advancing the iterator.

methods.name: taoverse.metagraph.miner_iterator.MinerIterator.set_miner_uids
documentation:
Updates the miner UIDs to iterate.

The iterator will be updated to the first miner uid that is greater than or equal to UID that would be next
returned by the iterator. This helps ensure that frequent updates to the miner_uids does not cause too much
churn in the sequence of UIDs returned by the iterator.

A thread safe infinite iterator to cyclically enumerate the current set of miner UIDs.

Why? To perform miner evaluations, the validator will enumerate through the miners in order to help ensure
each miner is evaluated at least once per epoch.

methods.name: taoverse.metagraph.metagraph_syncer.MetagraphSyncer.__init__
documentation:
Constructs a new MetagraphSyncer, that periodically refreshes metagraph defined in the config.

Args:
    subtensor (bt.subtensor): The subtensor used to fetch the metagraphs.
    config (Dict[int, int]): A mapping of netuid to the cadence (in seconds) to sync the metagraph.
    lite (bool): If the metagraphs should be synced as a lite metagraph (without detailed Weights).

methods.name: taoverse.metagraph.metagraph_syncer.MetagraphSyncer.do_initial_sync
documentation:
Performs an initial sync of all metagraphs.

Unlike regular syncs, this will not notify listeners of the updated metagraph.

methods.name: taoverse.metagraph.metagraph_syncer.MetagraphSyncer.register_listener
documentation:
Registers a listener to be notified when a metagraph for any netuid in netuids is updated.

The listener will be called from a different thread, so it must be thread-safe.

methods.name: taoverse.metagraph.metagraph_syncer.MetagraphSyncer.get_metagraph
documentation: Returns the last synced version of the metagraph for netuid.

methods.name: taoverse.metagraph.metagraph_syncer.MetagraphSyncer._notify_listeners
documentation: Notifies listeners of a new metagraph for netuid.

functions.name: taoverse.utilities.utils.run_in_subprocess
documentation:
Runs the provided function on a subprocess with 'ttl' seconds to complete.

Args:
    func (functools.partial): Function to be run.
    ttl (int): How long to try for in seconds.
    mode (str): Mode by which the multiprocessing context is obtained. Default to fork for pickling.

Returns:
    Any: The value returned by 'func'

functions.name: taoverse.utilities.utils.run_in_thread
documentation:
Runs the provided function on a thread with 'ttl' seconds to complete.

Args:
    func (functools.partial): Function to be run.
    ttl (int): How long to try for in seconds.

Returns:
    Any: The value returned by 'func'

functions.name: taoverse.utilities.utils.get_version
documentation:
Loads a version from the provided filepath or None if the file does not exist.

Args:
    filepath (str): Path to the version file.

functions.name: taoverse.utilities.utils.save_version
documentation: Saves a version to the provided filepath.

functions.name: taoverse.utilities.utils.random_date
documentation:
Return a random datetime between two datetimes.

Args:
    start (datetime): Start of the range, inclusive.
    end (datetime): End of the range, inclusive.
    seed (int): Optional Seed for the random number generator.

functions.name: taoverse.utilities.utils.fingerprint
documentation: Returns a fingerprint for a Dataclass or sequence of Dataclasses.

classes.name: taoverse.utilities.perf_monitor.PerfMonitor

methods.name: taoverse.utilities.perf_monitor.PerfMonitor.name
documentation: Returns the name of the operation being tracked.

methods.name: taoverse.utilities.perf_monitor.PerfMonitor.sample
documentation: Returns a context manager that will record the duration of the block it wraps.

methods.name: taoverse.utilities.perf_monitor.PerfMonitor.min
documentation: Returns the minimum duration recorded by the tracker in seconds.

methods.name: taoverse.utilities.perf_monitor.PerfMonitor.max
documentation: Returns the maximum duration recorded by the tracker in seconds.

methods.name: taoverse.utilities.perf_monitor.PerfMonitor.median
documentation: Returns the median duration recorded by the tracker in seconds.

methods.name: taoverse.utilities.perf_monitor.PerfMonitor.percentile
documentation: Returns the pth percentile duration recorded by the tracker in seconds.

methods.name: taoverse.utilities.perf_monitor.PerfMonitor.summary_str
documentation: Returns a string summarizing the performance of the tracked operation.

PerfMonitor is a context manager that tracks the performance of a block of code by taking several samples.

Example:
    tracker = PerfMonitor("MyOperation")
    for _ in range(10):
        with tracker.sample():
            // Do something

    print(tracker.summary_str())

classes.name: taoverse.utilities.enum_action.IntEnumAction
documentation: Argparse action for handling Enums

functions.name: taoverse.utilities.wandb.login
documentation: Logs in to wandb using the access token from the environment.

classes.name: taoverse.model.eval.task.EvalTask
documentation: Represents a task to evaluate a model on.

functions.name: taoverse.model.eval.normalization.normalize_score
documentation:
Normalizes a score based on the provided normalization method.

Args:
    score (float): The raw score to normalize.
    normalization_id (NormalizationId): The identifier of the normalization method to use.
    norm_kwargs (dict): Keyword arguments to pass to the normalization method.

Returns:
    float: The normalized score.

functions.name: taoverse.model.eval.normalization._normalize_inverse_exponential
documentation:
Normalizes between [0, 1] using an inverse exponential function.

Args:
    score (float): The raw score to normalize.
    ceiling (float): The maximum desirable score. Everything above this will be clipped.

Returns:
    float: The normalized score.

classes.name: taoverse.model.eval.normalization.NormalizationId
documentation: Enumeration of normalization methods.

Verifies a Hugging Face repo id is valid and returns it split into namespace and name.

classes.name: taoverse.model.model_updater.ModelUpdater

methods.name: taoverse.model.model_updater.ModelUpdater._get_metadata
documentation: Get metadata about a model by hotkey

methods.name: taoverse.model.model_updater.ModelUpdater.sync_model
documentation:
Updates local model for a hotkey if out of sync and returns if it was updated."

Args:
   hotkey (str): The hotkey of the model to sync.
   curr_block (int): The current block.
   force (bool): Whether to force a sync for this model, even if it's chain metadata hasn't changed.
   schedule_by_block (List[Tuple[int, List[Competition]]]): Which competitions are being run at a given block.

methods.name: taoverse.model.model_updater.ModelUpdater._validate_parameters
documentation:
Validate that parameters of a model are below the specified epsilon limits.

Parameters:
    base_model (transformers.PreTrainedModel): The base model instance.
    num_layers (int): Number of layers in the model to inspect.
    eps_soft (float): Calculate the percentage of layers above this norm
    eps_soft_percent_threshold (float): Threshold of percentage above eps_soft that will trigger a detection
    eps_hard (float): Hard limit for any norm
Checks if the currently tracked model for a hotkey matches what the miner committed to the chain.

functions.name: taoverse.model.storage.chain.chain_model_metadata_store.test_store_model_metadata
documentation: Verifies that the ChainModelMetadataStore can store data on the chain.
Can only commit data every ~20 minutes.

functions.name: taoverse.model.storage.chain.chain_model_metadata_store.test_retrieve_model_metadata

functions.name: taoverse.model.storage.chain.chain_model_metadata_store.test_roundtrip_model_metadata

methods.name: taoverse.model.model_tracker.ModelTracker.get_block_last_evaluated
documentation:
Returns the block of the most recent evaluation for a miner, across all competitions.

Args:
    hotkey (str): The miner's hotkey.
    
Returns:
    Optional[int]: The block of the most recent evaluation for the miner, or None if no evaluations have been made.

methods.name: taoverse.model.model_tracker.ModelTracker.on_hotkeys_updated
documentation: Notifies the tracker which hotkeys are currently being tracked on the metagraph.

methods.name: taoverse.model.model_tracker.ModelTracker.on_miner_model_updated
documentation:
Notifies the tracker that a miner has had their associated model updated.

Args:

    model_metadata (ModelMetadata): The latest model metadata of the miner.

methods.name: taoverse.model.model_tracker.ModelTracker.on_model_evaluated
documentation:
Notifies the tracker that a model has been evaluated.

Args:
    hotkey (str): The miner's hotkey.
    competition_id (int): The competition id for this eval result.
    result (EvalResult): The evaluation result.

Tracks model information for each miner.

Thread safe.

Computes the epsilon value.

Args:

Interface for a function to compute the current epsilon value.

An epsilon function linearly decays epsilon to a minimum epsilon over a defined number of blocks.

methods.name: taoverse.model.competition.competition_tracker.CompetitionTracker.record_competition_weights
documentation:
Records the weights from a new run of a competition, updating a moving average.

Args:
    competition_id (int): Which competition the weights are for.
    new_weights (torch.Tensor): Weights from the new run of the competition.
TODO: Consider a record_competition_results which then does additional processing into weights.

methods.name: taoverse.model.competition.competition_tracker.CompetitionTracker.reset_competitions
documentation:
Resets tracked competitions to only those identified.

Args:
    competition_ids (typing.Set[int]): Competition ids to continue tracking.

methods.name: taoverse.model.competition.competition_tracker.CompetitionTracker.get_subnet_weights
documentation:
Gets the weights that should be set on the subnet level based on all competitions.

Args:
    competitions (typing.List[Competition]): Competitions to calculate weights across.

Returns:
    torch.Tensor: Weights calculated across all specified competitions.

methods.name: taoverse.model.competition.competition_tracker.CompetitionTracker.get_competition_weights
documentation: Returns the current weights for a single competition

methods.name: taoverse.model.competition.competition_tracker.CompetitionTracker._resize
documentation:
Resizes the score tensor to the new number of neurons.

The new size must be greater than or equal to the current size.
